instructions: (Claude Code's built-in system prompt; see --bare mode docs)

queries.user_text:
You're debugging a real-codebase bug in `libucl-2` (project `libucl`, an open-source C/C++ project from the BugsC++ corpus).

The buggy source tree is on the host at `/Users/ibraheemamin/projects/COS484/chatdbgpro/data/workspaces/libucl-2/libucl/buggy-2` and is also mounted at `/work` inside a Linux/amd64 container named `bench-t4-7c2537ec64494db1a2f3` (gdb, the buggy binary, and all build deps live in that container; the binary is NOT runnable on this host directly).

Buggy binary inside the container: `/work/tests/.libs/test_basic`
Failing test invocation:           `/work/tests/.libs/test_basic ../tests/basic/13.in ../tests/basic.out`
Observed behavior:                  `exit_code:2`
Bug type:                          `other`
Language:                          `c`

How to investigate
------------------
* To READ source code, use Read / Grep / your normal tools — the source tree is at `/Users/ibraheemamin/projects/COS484/chatdbgpro/data/workspaces/libucl-2/libucl/buggy-2` (it's been added via `--add-dir`).
* To RUN the binary, run gdb, or execute anything else inside the build environment, use Bash with this template:

      docker exec -i -w /work bench-t4-7c2537ec64494db1a2f3 bash -c '<cmd>'

  e.g.
      docker exec -w /work bench-t4-7c2537ec64494db1a2f3 bash -c 'ls -la'
      docker exec -w /work bench-t4-7c2537ec64494db1a2f3 bash -c 'gdb -batch -ex run -ex bt --args /work/tests/.libs/test_basic ../tests/basic/13.in ../tests/basic.out'

  The container is dedicated to this case; it'll be torn down when this session ends, so don't worry about cleanup.

Final answer
------------
Identify the root cause in the source, propose both a local fix and a structural global fix.

Your final response MUST include three labelled paragraphs:

  ROOT CAUSE: <file:line and what is wrong>
  LOCAL FIX:  <minimal code change>
  GLOBAL FIX: <structural change preventing this CLASS of bug>

Do NOT modify the source files on disk. Just investigate and produce the diagnosis as your final assistant message.

tool_calls.call: docker exec -i -w /work bench-t4-7c2537ec64494db1a2f3 bash -c '/work/tests/.libs/test_basic ../tests/basic/13.in /tmp/out.txt 2>&1; echo "Exit code: $?"'

docker exec -i -w /work bench-t4-7c2537ec64494db1a2f3 bash -c 'export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/work/src/.libs:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH && /work/tests/.libs/test_basic ../tests/basic/13.in /tmp/out.txt 2>&1; echo "Exit code: $?"'

docker exec -i -w /work bench-t4-7c2537ec64494db1a2f3 bash -c 'export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/work/src/.libs:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH && gdb -batch -ex "run ../tests/basic/13.in /tmp/out.txt" -ex "bt" --args /work/tests/.libs/test_basic 2>&1 | head -100'

docker exec -i -w /work bench-t4-7c2537ec64494db1a2f3 bash -c 'export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/work/src/.libs:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH && /work/tests/.libs/test_basic ../tests/basic/13.in /tmp/out.txt 2>/tmp/err.txt; echo "Exit: $?"; cat /tmp/err.txt 2>&1; cat /tmp/out.txt 2>&1'
docker exec -i -w /work bench-t4-7c2537ec64494db1a2f3 bash -c 'ls -la tests/basic/13.in tests/.libs/test_basic'

docker exec -i -w /work/tests/.libs bench-t4-7c2537ec64494db1a2f3 bash -c 'export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/work/src/.libs:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH && ./test_basic ../basic/13.in /tmp/out.txt 2>&1; cat /tmp/out.txt 2>&1 || echo "No output file"'

python3 << 'EOF'
with open('/Users/ibraheemamin/projects/COS484/chatdbgpro/data/workspaces/libucl-2/libucl/buggy-2/tests/basic/13.in', 'r') as f:
    lines = f.readlines()
    line4 = lines[3]
    print(f"Line 4: {repr(line4)}")
    print(f"Length: {len(line4)}")
    if len(line4) >= 36:
        print(f"Character at column 36 (0-indexed 35): {repr(line4[35])}")
        print(f"Context around column 36: {repr(line4[30:40])}")
EOF

docker exec -i -w /work/tests/.libs bench-t4-7c2537ec64494db1a2f3 bash -c '
export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/work/src/.libs:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH

# Create a simple test file
cat > /tmp/test_macro.conf << "EOF"
test = 1;
.include(option="value") "/nonexistent"
EOF

./test_basic /tmp/test_macro.conf /tmp/out.txt 2>&1
'

docker exec -i -w /work/tests/.libs bench-t4-7c2537ec64494db1a2f3 bash -c '
export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/work/src/.libs:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH

# Create a simple test file
cat > /tmp/test_macro.conf << "EOF"

EOF

export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/work/src/.libs:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH

# Create a simple test file
cat > /tmp/test_macro.conf << "EOF"

EOF

./test_basic /tmp/test_macro.conf 2>&1
'

docker exec -i -w /work/tests/.libs bench-t4-7c2537ec64494db1a2f3 bash -c '
export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/work/src/.libs:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH

# Create a simple test file
cat > /tmp/test_macro.conf << "EOF"
.include(option=value) "/nonexistent"
EOF

./test_basic /tmp/test_macro.conf 2>&1
'

python3 << 'EOF'
# Simulate the parser logic for "option=\"value\""
input_str = 'option="value"'
obraces = 1  # opening paren already seen
ebraces = 0
state = 0
args_len = 0
c_ptr = 0

print(f"Processing: {input_str}")
print("Simulating parser logic:")

for i, char in enumerate(input_str):
    old_state = state
    old_args_len = args_len
    
    if state == 0:
        if char == '(':
            obraces += 1
        elif char == ')':
            ebraces += 1
        elif char == '"':
            state = 1
        
        if obraces == ebraces:
            state = 99
        else:
            args_len += 1
    elif state == 1:
        if char == '"' and (i == 0 or input_str[i-1] != '\\'):
            state = 0
        # NOTE: args_len is NOT incremented in state 1!
    
    print(f"  [{i}] '{char}': state {old_state}->{state}, args_len {old_args_len}->{args_len}, obraces={obraces}, ebraces={ebraces}")

print(f"\nFinal args_len: {args_len}")
print(f"Substring that would be parsed: {input_str[:args_len]}")
EOF
